title: swida sanguinea subsp. hungarica
intro: Swida sanguinea subsp. hungarica, also known as Hungarian dogwood or Hungarian dogwood shrub, is a subspecies of Swida sanguinea, a flowering plant native to Europe. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, primarily due to the presence of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The plant has been reported to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and boosting the immune system. In herbal preparations, Swida sanguinea subsp. hungarica is often used as a tincture, decoction, or tea, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat various conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues.
